channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Direct Loading of BaF Molecules with a Conveyor-Belt Magneto-optical Trap
item.description:
Author(s): Zixuan Zeng, Shoukang Yang, Shuhua Deng, and Bo Yan

We report the realization of a blue-detuned magneto-optical trap (BDM) of BaF molecules. The ($1+1$) type BDM and ($1+2$) type conveyor-belt MOT (CB-MOT) are explored. While the ($1+1$) BDM provides only a weak trapping force, the CB-MOT significantly compresses the molecular cloud, achieving a Gaus…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 073402] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zixuan Zeng, Shoukang Yang, Shuhua Deng, and Bo Yan

We report the realization of a blue-detuned magneto-optical trap (BDM) of BaF molecules. The (1+1) type BDM and (1+2) type conveyor-belt MOT (CB-MOT) are explored. While the (1+1) BDM provides only a weak trapping force, the CB-MOT significantly compresses the molecular cloud, achieving a Gaussian r…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 073402] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Direct Loading of BaF Molecules with a Conveyor-Belt Magneto-optical Trap
item.dc:creator: Zixuan Zeng, Shoukang Yang, Shuhua Deng, and Bo Yan
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Questioning the Cuprate Paradigm: Absence of Superfluid Density Loss in Several Overdoped Cuprates I
item.description:
Author(s): J. L. Tallon, J. G. Storey, J. W. Loram, Jianlin Luo, C. Bernhard, I. Kokanović, and J. R. Cooper

It is long established that overdoped cuprate superconductors experience a loss of superfluid density (SFD) with increasing doping, $p$, along with the decline in ${T}_{c}$. Such behavior is unconventional and suggests a depletion of the condensate by increasing pairbreaking or the growth of a secon…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076002] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): J. L. Tallon, J. G. Storey, J. W. Loram, Jianlin Luo, C. Bernhard, I. Kokanović, and J. R. Cooper

It is long established that overdoped cuprate superconductors experience a loss of superfluid density (SFD) with increasing doping, p, along with the decline in Tc. Such behavior is unconventional and suggests a depletion of the condensate by increasing pairbreaking or the growth of a second nonpair…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076002] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Questioning the Cuprate Paradigm: Absence of Superfluid Density Loss in Several Overdoped Cuprates I
item.dc:creator: J. L. Tallon, J. G. Storey, J. W. Loram, Jianlin Luo, C. Bernhard, I. Kokanović, and J. R. Cooper
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quintuplet Condensation in the Skyrmionic Insulator ${\mathrm{Cu}}_{2}{\mathrm{OSeO}}_{3}$ at Ultrahigh Magnetic Fields
item.description:
Author(s): T. Nomura, I. Rousochatzakis, O. Janson, M. Gen, X.-G. Zhou, Y. Ishii, S. Seki, Y. Kohama, and Y. H. Matsuda

Faraday rotation at ultrahigh magnetic fields uncovers magnon Bose–Einstein condensation in a chiral helimagnet


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076703] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): T. Nomura, I. Rousochatzakis, O. Janson, M. Gen, X.-G. Zhou, Y. Ishii, S. Seki, Y. Kohama, and Y. H. Matsuda

Faraday rotation at ultrahigh magnetic fields uncovers magnon Bose–Einstein condensation in a chiral helimagnet


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076703] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Quintuplet Condensation in the Skyrmionic Insulator ${\mathrm{Cu}}_{2}{\mathrm{OSeO}}_{3}$ at Ultrahigh Magnetic Fields
item.dc:creator: T. Nomura, I. Rousochatzakis, O. Janson, M. Gen, X.-G. Zhou, Y. Ishii, S. Seki, Y. Kohama, and Y. H. Matsuda

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Instantaneous Optical Selection Rule for Independent Control of Valley Currents
item.description:
Author(s): Wanzhu He, Xiaosong Zhu, Liang Li, Di Wu, Xiaotong Zhu, Pengfei Lan, and Peixiang Lu

We reveal an instantaneous optical valley selection rule that illuminates the coupling between the instantaneous optical chirality of the driving laser field and the chirality of valley systems. Building on this principle, we propose and demonstrate that a single chirality-separated optical field, i…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076902] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Wanzhu He, Xiaosong Zhu, Liang Li, Di Wu, Xiaotong Zhu, Pengfei Lan, and Peixiang Lu

We reveal an instantaneous optical valley selection rule that illuminates the coupling between the instantaneous optical chirality of the driving laser field and the chirality of valley systems. Building on this principle, we propose and demonstrate that a single chirality-separated optical field, i…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076902] Published Thu Feb 19, 2026
item.dc:title: Instantaneous Optical Selection Rule for Independent Control of Valley Currents
item.dc:creator: Wanzhu He, Xiaosong Zhu, Liang Li, Di Wu, Xiaotong Zhu, Pengfei Lan, and Peixiang Lu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: New Upper Bounds on Exotic Neutron-Spin–Electron-Spin Interactions via Neutron-Spin-Rotation Measurements in a Compensated Ferrimagnet
item.description:
Author(s): T. Mulkey, K. N. Lopez, C. D. Hughes, B. Hill, M. Van Meter, H. Wijeratne, J. C. Long, M. Sarsour, W. M. Snow, K. Li, R. Parajuli, S. Samiei, D. V. Baxter, M. Luxnat, Y. Zhang, C. Jiang, E. Stringfellow, J. Torres, and R. Hobbs

Slow neutrons are used to place new constraints on electron-neutron spin-spin interactions from a hypothetical boson over more than five orders of magnitude in mass.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 071801] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): T. Mulkey, K. N. Lopez, C. D. Hughes, B. Hill, M. Van Meter, H. Wijeratne, J. C. Long, M. Sarsour, W. M. Snow, K. Li, R. Parajuli, S. Samiei, D. V. Baxter, M. Luxnat, Y. Zhang, C. Jiang, E. Stringfellow, J. Torres, and R. Hobbs

Slow neutrons are used to place new constraints on electron-neutron spin-spin interactions from a hypothetical boson over more than five orders of magnitude in mass.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 071801] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.dc:title: New Upper Bounds on Exotic Neutron-Spin–Electron-Spin Interactions via Neutron-Spin-Rotation Measurements in a Compensated Ferrimagnet
item.dc:creator: T. Mulkey, K. N. Lopez, C. D. Hughes, B. Hill, M. Van Meter, H. Wijeratne, J. C. Long, M. Sarsour, W. M. Snow, K. Li, R. Parajuli, S. Samiei, D. V. Baxter, M. Luxnat, Y. Zhang, C. Jiang, E. Stringfellow, J. Torres, and R. Hobbs
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Particles and Fields

item.title: Atomic Regional Superfluids in Two-Dimensional Moiré Time Crystals
item.description:
Author(s): Weijie Liang, Weiping Zhang, and Keye Zhang

Moiré physics has transcended spatial dimensions, extending into synthetic domains and enabling novel quantum phenomena. We propose a theoretical model for a two-dimensional (2D) moiré time crystal formed by ultracold atoms, induced by periodic perturbations applied to a nonlattice trap. Our analysi…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 073401] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Weijie Liang, Weiping Zhang, and Keye Zhang

Moiré physics has transcended spatial dimensions, extending into synthetic domains and enabling novel quantum phenomena. We propose a theoretical model for a two-dimensional (2D) moiré time crystal formed by ultracold atoms, induced by periodic perturbations applied to a nonlattice trap. Our analysi…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 073401] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Atomic Regional Superfluids in Two-Dimensional Moiré Time Crystals
item.dc:creator: Weijie Liang, Weiping Zhang, and Keye Zhang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Coexisting Electronic Smectic Liquid Crystal and Superconductivity in a Si Square-Net Semimetal
item.description:
Author(s): Christopher J. Butler, Toshiya Ikenobe, Ming-Chun Jiang, Daigorou Hirai, Takahiro Yamada, Guang-Yu Guo, Ryotaro Arita, Tetsuo Hanaguri, and Zenji Hiroi

Electronic nematic and smectic liquid crystals are spontaneous symmetry-breaking phases that are seen to precede or coexist with enigmatic unconventional superconducting states in multiple classes of materials. In this Letter we describe scanning tunneling microscopy observations of a short ranged c…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076001] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Christopher J. Butler, Toshiya Ikenobe, Ming-Chun Jiang, Daigorou Hirai, Takahiro Yamada, Guang-Yu Guo, Ryotaro Arita, Tetsuo Hanaguri, and Zenji Hiroi

Electronic nematic and smectic liquid crystals are spontaneous symmetry-breaking phases that are seen to precede or coexist with enigmatic unconventional superconducting states in multiple classes of materials. In this Letter we describe scanning tunneling microscopy observations of a short ranged c…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076001] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Coexisting Electronic Smectic Liquid Crystal and Superconductivity in a Si Square-Net Semimetal
item.dc:creator: Christopher J. Butler, Toshiya Ikenobe, Ming-Chun Jiang, Daigorou Hirai, Takahiro Yamada, Guang-Yu Guo, Ryotaro Arita, Tetsuo Hanaguri, and Zenji Hiroi

item.title: Quantum Statistics and Self-Interference in Extended Colliders
item.description:
Author(s): Sai Satyam Samal, Smitha Vishveshwara, Yuval Gefen, and Jukka I. Väyrynen

Collision of quantum particles remains an effective way of probing their mutual statistics. Colliders based on quantum point contacts in quantum Hall edge states have been successfully used to probe the statistics of the underlying quantum particles. Notwithstanding the extensive theoretical work fo…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076301] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sai Satyam Samal, Smitha Vishveshwara, Yuval Gefen, and Jukka I. Väyrynen

Collision of quantum particles remains an effective way of probing their mutual statistics. Colliders based on quantum point contacts in quantum Hall edge states have been successfully used to probe the statistics of the underlying quantum particles. Notwithstanding the extensive theoretical work fo…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076301] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Statistics and Self-Interference in Extended Colliders
item.dc:creator: Sai Satyam Samal, Smitha Vishveshwara, Yuval Gefen, and Jukka I. Väyrynen

item.title: Interfacial Spin-Orbit Coupling Induced Room Temperature Ferromagnetic Insulator
item.description:
Author(s): Yuhao Hong, Shilin Hu, Ziyue Shen, Chao Deng, Xiaodong Zhang, Lei Wang, Long Wei, Qinghua Zhang, Lingfei Wang, Liang Si, Yulin Gan, Kai Chen, and Zhaoliang Liao

Fabricating room-temperature ferromagnetic insulators, which are crucial candidates for next-generation dissipation-free quantum and spintronic devices, remains a significant challenge. In this Letter, we report on the epitaxial synthesis of novel room-temperature ferromagnetic insulating thin films…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076302] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuhao Hong, Shilin Hu, Ziyue Shen, Chao Deng, Xiaodong Zhang, Lei Wang, Long Wei, Qinghua Zhang, Lingfei Wang, Liang Si, Yulin Gan, Kai Chen, and Zhaoliang Liao

Fabricating room-temperature ferromagnetic insulators, which are crucial candidates for next-generation dissipation-free quantum and spintronic devices, remains a significant challenge. In this Letter, we report on the epitaxial synthesis of novel room-temperature ferromagnetic insulating thin films…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076302] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Interfacial Spin-Orbit Coupling Induced Room Temperature Ferromagnetic Insulator
item.dc:creator: Yuhao Hong, Shilin Hu, Ziyue Shen, Chao Deng, Xiaodong Zhang, Lei Wang, Long Wei, Qinghua Zhang, Lingfei Wang, Liang Si, Yulin Gan, Kai Chen, and Zhaoliang Liao

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Oxygen Isotope Fingerprints of Electron-Phonon Coupling in ${\mathrm{SrVO}}_{3}$ Films
item.description:
Author(s): Gyanendra Singh, Xiaochun Huang, Mathieu Mirjolet, Salvador Pané, Thomas Lippert, Christof W. Schneider, and Josep Fontcuberta

Transition metals exemplify correlated electronic systems, where electron-electron ($e\text{−}e$) scattering often results in a quadratic temperature dependence of the electrical resistivity, $ρ(T)∝{T}^{2}$. In ${\mathrm{SrVO}}_{3}$ (SVO), a material with a $V\text{−}3{\mathrm{d}}^{1}$ electronic co…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076501] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gyanendra Singh, Xiaochun Huang, Mathieu Mirjolet, Salvador Pané, Thomas Lippert, Christof W. Schneider, and Josep Fontcuberta

Transition metals exemplify correlated electronic systems, where electron-electron (e−e) scattering often results in a quadratic temperature dependence of the electrical resistivity, ρ(T)∝T2. In SrVO3 (SVO), a material with a V−3d1 electronic configuration that ensures metallicity through narrow 3d−…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076501] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Oxygen Isotope Fingerprints of Electron-Phonon Coupling in ${\mathrm{SrVO}}_{3}$ Films
item.dc:creator: Gyanendra Singh, Xiaochun Huang, Mathieu Mirjolet, Salvador Pané, Thomas Lippert, Christof W. Schneider, and Josep Fontcuberta
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Vacancy Induced Expansion of Spin-Liquid Regime in the ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ Heisenberg Model
item.description:
Author(s): Soumyaranjan Dash, Anish Koley, and Sanjeev Kumar

We study the model for spin-$1/2$ ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ Heisenberg antiferromagnets on a square lattice in the presence of spin vacancies. In order to overcome the methodological challenges associated with analyzing models with magnetic frustration and inhomogeneities, we introduce a new semiclas…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076502] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Soumyaranjan Dash, Anish Koley, and Sanjeev Kumar

We study the model for spin-1/2 J1−J2 Heisenberg antiferromagnets on a square lattice in the presence of spin vacancies. In order to overcome the methodological challenges associated with analyzing models with magnetic frustration and inhomogeneities, we introduce a new semiclassical approach in whi…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076502] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Vacancy Induced Expansion of Spin-Liquid Regime in the ${J}_{1}\text{−}{J}_{2}$ Heisenberg Model
item.dc:creator: Soumyaranjan Dash, Anish Koley, and Sanjeev Kumar
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Bootstrapping Flatband Superconductors: Rigorous Lower Bounds on Superfluid Stiffness
item.description:
Author(s): Qiang Gao, Zhaoyu Han, and Eslam Khalaf

The superfluid stiffness fundamentally constrains the transition temperature of superconductors, especially in the strongly coupled regime. However, accurately determining this inherently quantum many-body property in microscopic models remains a significant challenge. In this Letter, we show how th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076503] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qiang Gao, Zhaoyu Han, and Eslam Khalaf

The superfluid stiffness fundamentally constrains the transition temperature of superconductors, especially in the strongly coupled regime. However, accurately determining this inherently quantum many-body property in microscopic models remains a significant challenge. In this Letter, we show how th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076503] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Bootstrapping Flatband Superconductors: Rigorous Lower Bounds on Superfluid Stiffness
item.dc:creator: Qiang Gao, Zhaoyu Han, and Eslam Khalaf
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Pseudocriticality in Antiferromagnetic Spin Chains
item.description:
Author(s): Sankalp Kumar, Sumiran Pujari, and Jonathan D’Emidio

Weak first-order pseudocriticality with approximate scale invariance has been observed in a variety of settings, including the intriguing case of deconfined criticality in $2+1$ dimensions. Recently, this has been interpreted as extremely slow flows (“walking behavior”) for real-valued couplings in …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076701] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sankalp Kumar, Sumiran Pujari, and Jonathan D’Emidio

Weak first-order pseudocriticality with approximate scale invariance has been observed in a variety of settings, including the intriguing case of deconfined criticality in 2+1 dimensions. Recently, this has been interpreted as extremely slow flows (“walking behavior”) for real-valued couplings in pr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076701] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Pseudocriticality in Antiferromagnetic Spin Chains
item.dc:creator: Sankalp Kumar, Sumiran Pujari, and Jonathan D’Emidio
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Violation of Local Reciprocity in Charge-Orbital Interconversion
item.description:
Author(s): Hisanobu Kashiki, Hiroki Hayashi, Dongwook Go, Yuriy Mokrousov, and Kazuya Ando

We demonstrate a violation of local reciprocity in the interconversion between charge and orbital currents. By investigating orbital torque and orbital pumping in W/Ni bilayers, we show that the charge-orbital interconversion in the bulk of the W layer exhibits opposite signs in the direct and inver…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076702] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hisanobu Kashiki, Hiroki Hayashi, Dongwook Go, Yuriy Mokrousov, and Kazuya Ando

We demonstrate a violation of local reciprocity in the interconversion between charge and orbital currents. By investigating orbital torque and orbital pumping in W/Ni bilayers, we show that the charge-orbital interconversion in the bulk of the W layer exhibits opposite signs in the direct and inver…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076702] Published Wed Feb 18, 2026
item.dc:title: Violation of Local Reciprocity in Charge-Orbital Interconversion
item.dc:creator: Hisanobu Kashiki, Hiroki Hayashi, Dongwook Go, Yuriy Mokrousov, and Kazuya Ando
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Extremely High Excitonic $g$ Factors in 2D Crystals by Alloy-Induced Admixing of Band States
item.description:
Author(s): Katarzyna Olkowska-Pucko et al.

Semiconducting transition metal dichalcogenide monolayer alloys provide a material platform with remarkable valley-tunable properties that can be manipulated on demand.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076901] Published Tue Feb 17, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Katarzyna Olkowska-Pucko et al.

Semiconducting transition metal dichalcogenide monolayer alloys provide a material platform with remarkable valley-tunable properties that can be manipulated on demand.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 076901] Published Tue Feb 17, 2026
item.dc:title: Extremely High Excitonic $g$ Factors in 2D Crystals by Alloy-Induced Admixing of Band States

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Composite Fermion Theory of Fractional Chern Insulator Stability
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaodong Hu, Ying Ran, and Di Xiao

A mean-field theory of fractional Chern insulators based on the dipole picture of composite fermions extends beyond the ideal-band limit.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066504] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaodong Hu, Ying Ran, and Di Xiao

A mean-field theory of fractional Chern insulators based on the dipole picture of composite fermions extends beyond the ideal-band limit.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066504] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Composite Fermion Theory of Fractional Chern Insulator Stability
item.dc:creator: Xiaodong Hu, Ying Ran, and Di Xiao

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Floquet Engineering Spin Triplet States in Unconventional Magnets
item.description:
Author(s): Pei-Hao Fu, Sayan Mondal, Jun-Feng Liu, Yukio Tanaka, and Jorge Cayao

High-frequency linearly polarized light generates spin-triplet densities and odd-frequency spin-triplet Cooper pairs in d-wave altermagnets, features absent in the static phase.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066703] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pei-Hao Fu, Sayan Mondal, Jun-Feng Liu, Yukio Tanaka, and Jorge Cayao

High-frequency linearly polarized light generates spin-triplet densities and odd-frequency spin-triplet Cooper pairs in d-wave altermagnets, features absent in the static phase.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066703] Published Fri Feb 13, 2026
item.dc:title: Floquet Engineering Spin Triplet States in Unconventional Magnets
item.dc:creator: Pei-Hao Fu, Sayan Mondal, Jun-Feng Liu, Yukio Tanaka, and Jorge Cayao

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Signatures of Quantum Phase Transitions in Driven Dissipative Spin Chains
item.description:
Author(s): Mostafa Ali, Naushad A. Kamar, Alireza Seif, and Mohammad Maghrebi

Open driven quantum systems have defined a powerful paradigm of nonequilibrium phases and phase transitions; however, quantum phase transitions are generically not expected in this setting due to the decohering effect of dissipation. In this Letter, we consider a quantum Ising model subject to bulk …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 060404] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mostafa Ali, Naushad A. Kamar, Alireza Seif, and Mohammad Maghrebi

Open driven quantum systems have defined a powerful paradigm of nonequilibrium phases and phase transitions; however, quantum phase transitions are generically not expected in this setting due to the decohering effect of dissipation. In this Letter, we consider a quantum Ising model subject to bulk …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 060404] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Signatures of Quantum Phase Transitions in Driven Dissipative Spin Chains
item.dc:creator: Mostafa Ali, Naushad A. Kamar, Alireza Seif, and Mohammad Maghrebi
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Universal Crossover in the Three-Channel Charge Kondo Model at High Transparency
item.description:
Author(s): Nicolas Paris, Nicolas Dupuis, and Christophe Mora

Quantum impurity models provide a central framework for correlated electron physics, with quantum dots enabling controlled experimental realizations. While their weak-coupling behavior is well understood through mappings to Kondo Hamiltonians, the opposite regime of highly transparent contacts has l…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066501] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nicolas Paris, Nicolas Dupuis, and Christophe Mora

Quantum impurity models provide a central framework for correlated electron physics, with quantum dots enabling controlled experimental realizations. While their weak-coupling behavior is well understood through mappings to Kondo Hamiltonians, the opposite regime of highly transparent contacts has l…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066501] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Universal Crossover in the Three-Channel Charge Kondo Model at High Transparency

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quasi-One-Dimensional Spin Excitations in the Iron Pnictide ${\mathrm{NaFe}}_{0.53}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{0.47}\mathrm{As}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yifan Wang, David W. Tam, Weiyi Wang, R. A. Ewings, J. Ross Stewart, Masaaki Matsuda, Chongde Cao, Changle Liu, Rong Yu, Pengcheng Dai, and Yu Song

Spectroscopic measurements in model 1D correlated systems offer insights for understanding their two-dimensional counterparts, which include the cuprate and iron pnictide/chalcogenide superconductors. A major challenge is the identification of such correlated systems with dominantly 1D physics. In t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066503] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yifan Wang, David W. Tam, Weiyi Wang, R. A. Ewings, J. Ross Stewart, Masaaki Matsuda, Chongde Cao, Changle Liu, Rong Yu, Pengcheng Dai, and Yu Song

Spectroscopic measurements in model 1D correlated systems offer insights for understanding their two-dimensional counterparts, which include the cuprate and iron pnictide/chalcogenide superconductors. A major challenge is the identification of such correlated systems with dominantly 1D physics. In t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066503] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Quasi-One-Dimensional Spin Excitations in the Iron Pnictide ${\mathrm{NaFe}}_{0.53}{\mathrm{Cu}}_{0.47}\mathrm{As}$
item.dc:creator: Yifan Wang, David W. Tam, Weiyi Wang, R. A. Ewings, J. Ross Stewart, Masaaki Matsuda, Chongde Cao, Changle Liu, Rong Yu, Pengcheng Dai, and Yu Song
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Phononic Casimir Effect in Planar Materials
item.description:
Author(s): Pablo Rodriguez-Lopez, Dai-Nam Le, and Lilia M. Woods

The phononic Casimir effect between planar objects is investigated by deriving a formalism from the quantum partition function of the system following multiscattering approach. This fluctuation-induced coupling is mediated by phonons modeled as an effective elastic medium. We find that excitations w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066905] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pablo Rodriguez-Lopez, Dai-Nam Le, and Lilia M. Woods

The phononic Casimir effect between planar objects is investigated by deriving a formalism from the quantum partition function of the system following multiscattering approach. This fluctuation-induced coupling is mediated by phonons modeled as an effective elastic medium. We find that excitations w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066905] Published Thu Feb 12, 2026
item.dc:title: Phononic Casimir Effect in Planar Materials
item.dc:creator: Pablo Rodriguez-Lopez, Dai-Nam Le, and Lilia M. Woods
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Cavity Controls Core-to-Core Resonant Inelastic X-Ray Scattering
item.description:
Author(s): S.-X. Wang, Z.-Q. Zhao, X.-Y. Wang, T.-J. Li, Y. Su, Y. Uemura, F. Alves Lima, A. Khadiev, B.-H. Wang, J. M. Ablett, J-P. Rueff, H.-C. Wang, O. J. L. Fox, W.-B. Li, L.-F. Zhu, and X.-C. Huang

X-ray cavity quantum optics with inner-shell transitions has been limited by the spectral overlap between resonant and continuum states. Here, we report the first experimental demonstration of cavity-controlled core-to-core resonant inelastic x-ray scattering (RIXS). We suppress the absorption-edge …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 063601] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): S.-X. Wang, Z.-Q. Zhao, X.-Y. Wang, T.-J. Li, Y. Su, Y. Uemura, F. Alves Lima, A. Khadiev, B.-H. Wang, J. M. Ablett, J-P. Rueff, H.-C. Wang, O. J. L. Fox, W.-B. Li, L.-F. Zhu, and X.-C. Huang

X-ray cavity quantum optics with inner-shell transitions has been limited by the spectral overlap between resonant and continuum states. Here, we report the first experimental demonstration of cavity-controlled core-to-core resonant inelastic x-ray scattering (RIXS). We suppress the absorption-edge …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 063601] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Cavity Controls Core-to-Core Resonant Inelastic X-Ray Scattering
item.dc:creator: S.-X. Wang, Z.-Q. Zhao, X.-Y. Wang, T.-J. Li, Y. Su, Y. Uemura, F. Alves Lima, A. Khadiev, B.-H. Wang, J. M. Ablett, J-P. Rueff, H.-C. Wang, O. J. L. Fox, W.-B. Li, L.-F. Zhu, and X.-C. Huang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Parity Breaking and Sublattice Dichotomy in Monolayer FeSe Superconductor
item.description:
Author(s): Cui Ding, Zhipeng Xu, Xiaotong Jiao, Yinqi Hu, Wenxuan Zhao, Lexian Yang, Kun Jiang, Lili Wang, Jin-Feng Jia, Jiangping Hu, and Qi-Kun Xue

A unit cell represents the smallest repeating structure in solid-state physics and serves as the fundamental building block of a material. In iron-based superconductors, each unit cell contains two iron atoms, which form two sublattices in the two-dimensional iron layers. Under normal circumstances,…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066502] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Cui Ding, Zhipeng Xu, Xiaotong Jiao, Yinqi Hu, Wenxuan Zhao, Lexian Yang, Kun Jiang, Lili Wang, Jin-Feng Jia, Jiangping Hu, and Qi-Kun Xue

A unit cell represents the smallest repeating structure in solid-state physics and serves as the fundamental building block of a material. In iron-based superconductors, each unit cell contains two iron atoms, which form two sublattices in the two-dimensional iron layers. Under normal circumstances,…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066502] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Parity Breaking and Sublattice Dichotomy in Monolayer FeSe Superconductor
item.dc:creator: Cui Ding, Zhipeng Xu, Xiaotong Jiao, Yinqi Hu, Wenxuan Zhao, Lexian Yang, Kun Jiang, Lili Wang, Jin-Feng Jia, Jiangping Hu, and Qi-Kun Xue

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Magnetoelastic Coupling-Driven Chiral Spin Textures: A Skyrmion-Antiskyrmion-like Array
item.description:
Author(s): Gyungchoon Go and Se Kwon Kim

We theoretically demonstrate that sufficiently strong magnetoelastic coupling can change the ground state of otherwise uniform spin systems to chiral spin configurations. More specifically, we show that a periodic array of chiral spin textures can spontaneously emerge in a two-dimensional ferromagne…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066702] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Gyungchoon Go and Se Kwon Kim

We theoretically demonstrate that sufficiently strong magnetoelastic coupling can change the ground state of otherwise uniform spin systems to chiral spin configurations. More specifically, we show that a periodic array of chiral spin textures can spontaneously emerge in a two-dimensional ferromagne…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066702] Published Wed Feb 11, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetoelastic Coupling-Driven Chiral Spin Textures: A Skyrmion-Antiskyrmion-like Array
item.dc:creator: Gyungchoon Go and Se Kwon Kim

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Storage with Flat Bands
item.description:
Author(s): Carlo Danieli, Jie Liu, Rudolf A. Römer, and Rodrigo A. Vicencio

The realization of robust quantum storage devices relies on the ability to generate long-lived, spatially localized states. In this Letter, we introduce a method for the targeted creation of compact excitations in flat-band lattices. By injecting in-plane radiation waves from the system’s edge and a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066302] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Carlo Danieli, Jie Liu, Rudolf A. Römer, and Rodrigo A. Vicencio

The realization of robust quantum storage devices relies on the ability to generate long-lived, spatially localized states. In this Letter, we introduce a method for the targeted creation of compact excitations in flat-band lattices. By injecting in-plane radiation waves from the system’s edge and a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066302] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Storage with Flat Bands
item.dc:creator: Carlo Danieli, Jie Liu, Rudolf A. Römer, and Rodrigo A. Vicencio

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Marginal Metals and Kosterlitz-Thouless-Type Phase Transition in Disordered Altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Chang-An Li, Bo Fu, Huaiming Guo, Björn Trauzettel, and Song-Bo Zhang

Altermagnetism, a recently discovered magnetic phase characterized by spin-split bands without net magnetization, has emerged as a promising platform for novel physics and potential applications. However, its stability against disorder—ubiquitous in real materials—remains poorly understood. Here, we…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066303] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chang-An Li, Bo Fu, Huaiming Guo, Björn Trauzettel, and Song-Bo Zhang

Altermagnetism, a recently discovered magnetic phase characterized by spin-split bands without net magnetization, has emerged as a promising platform for novel physics and potential applications. However, its stability against disorder—ubiquitous in real materials—remains poorly understood. Here, we…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066303] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Marginal Metals and Kosterlitz-Thouless-Type Phase Transition in Disordered Altermagnets
item.dc:creator: Chang-An Li, Bo Fu, Huaiming Guo, Björn Trauzettel, and Song-Bo Zhang

item.title: Ultrasensitive Magnetometer Based on Cusp Points of the Photon-Magnon Synchronization Mode
item.description:
Author(s): Xinlin Mi, Jinwei Rao, Lijun Yan, Xudong Wang, Bingbing Lyu, Bimu Yao, Shishen Yan, and Lihui Bai

Ultrasensitive magnetometers based on spin resonances have led to remarkable achievements. However, the field responsivities of these spin resonances are inherently constrained by these particles’ gyromagnetic ratios, such as the electron, with a constant gyromagnetic ratio of ${γ}_{e}=2π×28\text{ }…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066701] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xinlin Mi, Jinwei Rao, Lijun Yan, Xudong Wang, Bingbing Lyu, Bimu Yao, Shishen Yan, and Lihui Bai

Ultrasensitive magnetometers based on spin resonances have led to remarkable achievements. However, the field responsivities of these spin resonances are inherently constrained by these particles’ gyromagnetic ratios, such as the electron, with a constant gyromagnetic ratio of γe=2π×28  GHz/T. Here,…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066701] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Ultrasensitive Magnetometer Based on Cusp Points of the Photon-Magnon Synchronization Mode
item.dc:creator: Xinlin Mi, Jinwei Rao, Lijun Yan, Xudong Wang, Bingbing Lyu, Bimu Yao, Shishen Yan, and Lihui Bai

item.title: Pressure-Tunable Hyperbolic Plasmons in Black Phosphorus Films
item.description:
Author(s): Yuwei Liu, Chong Wang, Junwei Ma, Yuqing Zheng, Wenqi Bi, Hao Sun, Xiangkai Meng, Shenyang Huang, Xiang Li, Hugen Yan, and Yugui Yao

High-pressure environments provide a unique platform for tuning quantum phenomena, yet their applications in plasmonics remain underexplored. Here, we investigate the pressure-induced evolution of plasmons in black phosphorus films using infrared spectroscopy. Continuous pressure tuning of anisotrop…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066902] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuwei Liu, Chong Wang, Junwei Ma, Yuqing Zheng, Wenqi Bi, Hao Sun, Xiangkai Meng, Shenyang Huang, Xiang Li, Hugen Yan, and Yugui Yao

High-pressure environments provide a unique platform for tuning quantum phenomena, yet their applications in plasmonics remain underexplored. Here, we investigate the pressure-induced evolution of plasmons in black phosphorus films using infrared spectroscopy. Continuous pressure tuning of anisotrop…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 066902] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Pressure-Tunable Hyperbolic Plasmons in Black Phosphorus Films
item.dc:creator: Yuwei Liu, Chong Wang, Junwei Ma, Yuqing Zheng, Wenqi Bi, Hao Sun, Xiangkai Meng, Shenyang Huang, Xiang Li, Hugen Yan, and Yugui Yao

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Unlocking Hidden Topological Multistability via Biphasic Correlated Order Evolution
item.description:
Author(s): Jin-Bing Wu, Zhenghao Guo, Baoming Shi, Daoxing Luo, Lei Zhang, Yan-Qing Lu, and Wei Hu

Topological multistability reflects the complexity of structure evolution inside ordered condensed matter. For a given thermodynamic system, the actual attained stable states decline sharply compared with the theoretical anticipation, which severely restricts the diversity of the material structure …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 068101] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jin-Bing Wu, Zhenghao Guo, Baoming Shi, Daoxing Luo, Lei Zhang, Yan-Qing Lu, and Wei Hu

Topological multistability reflects the complexity of structure evolution inside ordered condensed matter. For a given thermodynamic system, the actual attained stable states decline sharply compared with the theoretical anticipation, which severely restricts the diversity of the material structure …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 068101] Published Tue Feb 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Unlocking Hidden Topological Multistability via Biphasic Correlated Order Evolution
item.dc:creator: Jin-Bing Wu, Zhenghao Guo, Baoming Shi, Daoxing Luo, Lei Zhang, Yan-Qing Lu, and Wei Hu
